insight: Rule operators run BEFORE the attached group is evaluated, and a rule-level `then deny` throws at the end of the rule's operator execution, so the group's `require variable` and operators never run. For type-gated groups (require variable {rule_type} sign), all `then` operators must live inside the group and the rule must stay bare (match + group only). {rule_type} does resolve inside group-level `require variable` on current code.

The groups skill note "type filtering must be done at the rule level" is incomplete: a group CAN be type-gated with `require variable {rule_type} <chat|command|sign|book|anvil|tag>` since {rule_type} resolves inside group require checks. Alternative for sign-only rules: place them in rules/sign.rs, or use `ignore type chat|command|book|anvil|tag` on a global.rs rule (ignore type is pipe-separated and only allowed in global.rs).
